# Analysis of Optional Work and its Benefits
**By Julia Pate and Olivia Baird**

---

## 1. Analysis Summary
In this project, I explored the data from the COMP110 class to understand the relationship between optional work offered to the students and the effect it has on students and their confidence in class.
* **Data Processing:** Filtering the dataset for revelant timeframes.
* **Statistical Analysis:** Calculating the mean and correlation of key metrics.
* **Interpretation:** Evaluating how optional practice impacts overall outcome.

---

## 2. Visualizations
### Visualization 1: [Pre-Lesson Video Effectiveness]
![Chart 1](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/juliapate/finalprojectunc/main/static/imgs/chart1.png)

*This chart shows how the more videos watched by students and the more optional work, the more effective they become. It supports the idea that more opional work would be beneficial for students.*

### Visualization 2: [Optional Work Impact on Class Understanding]
![Chart 2](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/juliapate/finalprojectunc/main/static/imgs/chart2.png)

*This chart shows how students who use the videos have a similar understanding of the class and what they are learning. It supports the idea that students are having an understanding of the class content when they use the videos.*

### Visualization 3: [Grade That Benefits from Optional Work Most]
![Chart 3](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/juliapate/finalprojectunc/main/static/imgs/chart3.png)

*This chart shows how the younger students not yet accustomed to college like the optional practice and it helps to provide them with guidance throughtout the class to ensure that they do their best. It supports the idea that students with less college experience which is a majority of the students benefit from the optional work.*

---

## 3. Final Conclusions
# Based on the analysis conducted in our Juypter Notebook, We have reached the following conclusion:

Through our analysis and the graphs made from our code, we were able to analyze the data and find that there is a small correlation between the optional course resources and student learning outcomes. The main piece of data that was supported is from the first graph, which shows that a large portion of students who use the pre-lecture videos find them to be highly effective, with the data trending upward. While the videos and optional work are popular among students, the correlation between their effectiveness and the understanding students have of the course is more of a straight line pattern, and implies that students are reaching an understanding regardless of their use of the optional materials and videos. When looking at the third graph, we see that younger students, specifically freshmen and sophomores, find these optional resources more helpful than the juniors and seniors do. This helped to show us that the optional work is a resource that helps students new to the school build confidence in the coding aspect of the class. 

Based on the results that we have found, the recommendation we have is to implement an optional practice for the students to use, containing previous exam questions and additional practice videos. Our recommendation is supported by the data from the survey because even though some students can do well without the optional work, most of the other students view them as an effective study method and learning process, and use them to help fulfill the goal of reducing stress and feeling confident before tests and for the class overall. To make this idea more formal, we could focus on creating optional modules/practice for the first half of next semester to target the younger students who benefit and see how much it helps them, and if it is good, then we can continue to use it in the semesters ahead. We could also use a system that asks students for feedback right after students submit their work, so that they can get explanations for their mistakes and not have to reach out to the teacher or TA's as much.

There are several trade-offs and stakeholder impacts to make sure we keep in mind when making this change. This would require the TA's to invest some initial time into making the practice work, videos, and questions, and ensuring that the old quiz questions can still be used. Then they would have to upload them to the class site so that students can use them. There is also a chance that students may feel like they are pressured to do the optional work even if they may not need it, which could increase students' workload and stress, which isn't the goal. Finally, there is a continuing cost of maintaining the optional work as a resource because the materials must be updated frequently to match the course content and the speed at which the course is going, or they could lead to confusion in the class, which leads to more questions from students.
